title: IEC 61511 Clause 11.7.6 SIS-BPCS separation + SIS override and bypass logging with audit-trail tamper-detection
exclusions: Excludes operator-authorised proof-test bypasses recorded in the proof-test procedure (UC-22.63.4 tracks these). Excludes SIS instances where SIS and BPCS are deliberately integrated (rare and requires independent safety-case attestation per IEC 61511 Clause 11.2.10).
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.63.3` archived hourly to `audit_evidence` with `iec_clause="IEC-61511-Cl-11-7-6"`. Every override / bypass / write-to-SIS event pins (sis_id, event_type, operator_id, authorised_by, business_justification, duration_authorised_min, duration_actual_min, audit_evidence_ref, risk_score).

compliance.assurance_rationale: IEC 61511-1 Clause 11.2.10 requires the SIS to be independent of the BPCS (Basic Process Control System) in technology, hardware, and software to the maximum reasonable extent. Clause 11.7.6 requires that overrides and bypasses of safety functions be logged with operator-authorisation, business-justification, and time-bounded by procedure. This UC enforces both limbs: (i) any cross-talk between BPCS and SIS that is not in the documented exception register is flagged, and (ii) every override / bypass is reconciled against the procedure-mandated authorisation and time-bound.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates SIS-BPCS separation and proper authorisation + time-bound of overrides and bypasses.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search archived to `audit_evidence`, the `SIS Override and Bypass Register` dashboard, the per-SIS override-procedure document, the operator-authorisation logs.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Clause 11.7.6 specifically calls out maintenance overrides and bypasses as requiring procedures, authorisation, and time-bounding. This UC operationalises Clause 11.7.6 by reconciling every override / bypass event against the documented procedure, authorisation, justification, and elapsed-duration vs authorised-duration.
compliance.controlObjective: Provides the override-and-bypass-discipline limb of Clause 11.7.6.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An operator places a SIF in override for 60 minutes authorised but leaves the override engaged for 300 minutes without a re-authorisation. UC-22.63.3 MUST flag with risk_score=95 once the authorised window expires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: An operator places the SIF in override with a documented work-permit reference, the authorisation is signed by the shift supervisor, and the override is cleared within the authorised 60-minute window with a closure note. UC-22.63.3 MUST record risk_score=0.

dataSources: SIS logic-solver logs ingested via OPC-UA / Modbus / dedicated SIS export (e.g., Siemens F-CPU / Honeywell Safety Manager / HIMA HIMax / Emerson DeltaV SIS / Yokogawa ProSafe-RS) to Splunk via the Splunk OT Security Add-on or vendor TA. Engineering workstation logs (operator authorisations, work-permit references). Document-management system events for procedure references. KV Store `iec_61511_sis_override_procedure_lookup` (sis_id, sif_id, procedure_ref, max_authorised_duration_min, escalation_authority).
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk OT Security Add-on (Splunkbase 6849), Splunk Add-on for OPC UA (Splunkbase 4734).
spl:
index=ot_sis sourcetype IN ("sis:override","sis:bypass","sis:logic_solver") event_type IN ("override_start","override_end","bypass_start","bypass_end")
| transaction sis_id sif_id maxspan=24h startswith=eval(event_type IN ("override_start","bypass_start")) endswith=eval(event_type IN ("override_end","bypass_end"))
| eval duration_actual_min = duration / 60
| eval still_open = if(eventcount = 1, 1, 0)
| lookup iec_61511_sis_override_procedure_lookup sis_id, sif_id OUTPUT procedure_ref, max_authorised_duration_min, escalation_authority
| eval authorisation_present = if(isnotnull(authorised_by) AND authorised_by != "", 1, 0)
| eval justification_present = if(isnotnull(business_justification) AND business_justification != "", 1, 0)
| eval permit_ref_present = if(isnotnull(work_permit_ref) AND work_permit_ref != "", 1, 0)
| eval duration_overrun = if(isnotnull(duration_actual_min) AND duration_actual_min > max_authorised_duration_min, 1, 0)
| eval procedure_missing = if(isnull(procedure_ref), 1, 0)
| eval still_open_overrun = if(still_open = 1 AND ((now() - _time) / 60) > max_authorised_duration_min, 1, 0)
| eval risk_score = case(
    authorisation_present = 0, 100,
    still_open_overrun = 1, 100,
    duration_overrun = 1, 95,
    justification_present = 0, 85,
    permit_ref_present = 0, 75,
    procedure_missing = 1, 70,
    still_open = 1, 30,
    1=1, 0)
| where risk_score > 0
| sort 0 -risk_score, -duration_actual_min
| table sis_id sif_id event_type operator_id authorised_by business_justification work_permit_ref duration_actual_min max_authorised_duration_min still_open risk_score
description: Reconciles every SIS override / bypass event against the IEC 61511-1 Clause 11.7.6 discipline: (i) authorisation by a procedure-authorised operator, (ii) business-justification recorded, (iii) work-permit reference linked, (iv) duration within the procedure-mandated maximum, and (v) override closed at the end of the authorised window. Critical conditions: an override without an authorising operator (risk_score=100), or an override still open past its authorised duration (risk_score=100 — operator-discipline breach with direct safety-function-impairment risk).
value: IEC 61511 Clause 11.7.6 is the single most commonly cited finding in process-safety regulator audits (OSHA PSM CCNs, COMAH inspector reports, Seveso enforcement notices). 'Operators leave overrides engaged for shifts at a time' is the canonical post-incident finding. The audit-trail produced by this UC defends the operating organisation in a regulator-investigation and provides leading-indicator metrics to the Plant Safety Manager (overrides-engaged-time per shift, escalation-rate to shift supervisor) that correlate strongly with safety performance.
implementation: (1) Wire the SIS logic-solver to Splunk via vendor TA (Siemens F-CPU TA / Honeywell SM TA / HIMA HIMax TA / Emerson DeltaV SIS TA / Yokogawa ProSafe-RS TA). (2) Maintain `iec_61511_sis_override_procedure_lookup` with per-SIF maximum-authorised durations (typically 60 min for in-shift, 480 min for shift-spanning with shift-supervisor escalation). (3) Schedule UC-22.63.3 every 30 minutes; route `risk_score = 100` (open-overrun) to Shift Supervisor + Functional Safety Manager at severity P1, paging.
visualization: (Panel 1) Live open-override register with elapsed time. (Panel 2) Override-duration distribution. (Panel 3) Operator-discipline trend by team. (Panel 4) Procedure-coverage status.

references.title: IEC 61511-1:2016 Clause 11.7.6 — Bypasses, overrides, maintenance forces
IEC 61511-1:2016 Clause 11.2.10 — Separation of SIS from BPCS
OSHA Process Safety Management standard 29 CFR 1910.119

knownFalsePositives:
1. **Boot / startup transient**: a logic-solver reboot can briefly register override-start without a corresponding operator action — suppress with `boot_time_within_300s`. 2. **Proof-test bypass (UC-22.63.4)**: a bypass that is part of a documented proof-test procedure — load `proof_test_ref` and exclude from this UC's pipeline; UC-22.63.4 monitors proof-tests. 3. **Operator-on-leave handoff**: a long-running override that crosses shifts may be re-authorised by the incoming shift's supervisor — load `re_authorisation_chain_ref`.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `iec_61511_22_63_3_exceptions` with the Functional Safety Manager + Plant Manager documented approval, an explicit sis_id + sif_id + time-window scope, an expiry date, and a written justification.
equipmentModels: Schneider Electric Modicon Quantum Safety / Tricon CX
reviewer: Functional Safety Manager + Shift Supervisor + Plant Operations Manager
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- A vendor-specific TA for the deployed SIS logic-solver platform (Siemens / Honeywell / HIMA / Emerson / Yokogawa / Schneider).
- An operator-authorisation system (typically the SIS engineering workstation or a paper / electronic permit-to-work system).

## Step 1 - Override-procedure KV Store

```ini
[iec_61511_sis_override_procedure_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.sis_id = string
field.sif_id = string
field.procedure_ref = string
field.max_authorised_duration_min = number
field.escalation_authority = string
```

Seed typical entries:

```
sis_id,sif_id,procedure_ref,max_authorised_duration_min,escalation_authority
SIS-CRACKER-A,SIF-HHL-1,PROC-001-A,60,Shift-Supervisor
SIS-CRACKER-A,SIF-HPHL-2,PROC-001-A,30,Plant-Manager
SIS-COMPRESSOR-B,SIF-VIB-1,PROC-002-B,120,Shift-Supervisor
```

## Step 2 - Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.63.3: SIS Override and Bypass Register]
cron_schedule = */30 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -2h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.63.3,reg=IEC-61511,clause=IEC-61511-Cl-11-7-6
alert.severity = 5
```

## Step 3 - Functional-safety-specific notes

- **Force counters**: many SIS platforms (Siemens F-CPU, HIMA) expose 'force counters' as Modbus tags — surface these as `sis:force_counter` for engineering verification.
- **Plant-Manager escalation**: for SIL-3 SIF overrides, the escalation_authority should typically be the Plant Manager, not the Shift Supervisor — calibrate per the plant's safety-case.
grandmaExplanation: Sometimes operators must temporarily turn off part of a safety system to do maintenance. We track every time this happens, make sure the right people approved it, that there is a written reason and time limit, and that it is turned back on within the allowed time.
